steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Liu et al. concluded that these alloys' toughness differs from most HEAs and MEAs — meaning their toughness behaves unusually. The passage notes that typical HEAs and MEAs see toughness fall as temperature drops. A choice that shows the opposite pattern (toughness rising as temperature falls) would support the conclusion of unusual behavior.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘CrCoNi outperformed CrMnFeCoNi at 20 kelvins in both initiation toughness and propagation toughness even though it is composed of fewer elements.’: Compares the two alloys against each other at one temperature point but does not describe how either behaves across temperatures to show unusual toughness behavior. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Both CrMnFeCoNi and CrCoNi showed substantially higher propagation toughness than initiation toughness throughout the range from 20 kelvins to 293 kelvins.’: Contradicted by the graph: at 77 K, initiation and propagation values converge for both alloys, so 'substantially higher throughout' is false. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘The changes in initiation toughness and propagation toughness were greater between 20 kelvins and 77 kelvins than between 77 kelvins and 198 kelvins for both CrMnFeCoNi and CrCoNi.’: Contradicted for CrCoNi initiation toughness, where the drop from 77 K to 198 K exceeds the drop from 20 K to 77 K. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘For both CrMnFeCoNi and CrCoNi, initiation toughness and propagation toughness were lower at 293 kelvins than at 20 kelvins.’: Shows that both alloys' toughness is higher at cryogenic temperatures than at room temperature — the opposite of the typical HEA/MEA pattern, directly supporting the conclusion of unusual behavior. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
